channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's FBI pick has plans to reshape the bureau. This is what Kash Patel has said he wants to do
item.description: Kash Patel has been well-known for years within Donald Trump’s orbit as a loyal supporter who shares the president-elect’s skepticism of the FBI and intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some Democrats are frustrated over Joe Biden reversing course and pardoning his son Hunter
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Already reeling from their November defeats, Democrats now are grappling with President Joe Biden’s pardoning of his son for federal crimes, with some calling the move misguided and unwise after the party spent years slamming Donald Trump as a threat to democracy who disregarded the l
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday threatened to unleash “hell” in the Middle East if hostages held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration Jan.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says Trump wouldn't 'let go' of Hunter Biden in defending pardon
item.description: The White House on Monday defended President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter Biden after insisting for more than a year that he wouldn’t do so.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the rising Democratic populist leaders to watch
item.description: A group of Democrats on the left are searching for a new standard-bearer to put forward an economic populist agenda for America after extensive losses in the November election.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California bill would allow public university admission priority for slaves' descendants
item.description: California lawmakers are set to consider a bill that would require the state's public universities to give admission priority to the descendants of slaves.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Trân Nguyễn And Olga R. Rodriguez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: President Joe Biden has pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family members.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader Ben Wikler enters the race to lead the national party
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Ben Wikler, chair of the Democratic Party in Wisconsin, said Sunday he has joined the race to lead the national party after an election that swept Donald Trump and Republicans to power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: President Biden gives remarks for World AIDS Day
item.description: The White House is holding an event Sunday to commemorate World AIDS Day with survivors, their families, and advocates.
item.dc:creator: Mike Costanza, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump picks longtime ally Kash Patel to replace current FBI director
item.description: The president-elect plans to replace current FBI Director Christopher Wray with Patel, a close ally of the president-elect and former national security aide, has berated the Justice Department and the news media.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: President-elect Trump plans to nominate Kash Patel to serve as head of the FBI, elevating a staunch ally and controversial figure who sparked internal battles during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: NYT publishes email from Pete Hegseth's mom
item.description: The Times noted that Penelope Hegseth described their publishing of the email as "disgusting" and she apologized to her son in a follow-up email.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats look to governors to lead them to promised land
item.description: Democrats are looking to the governors of blue states as both a line of defense against the incoming Trump administration and a promising bench for the party in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's Trudeau says he had an 'excellent conversation' with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: The Canadian prime minister's meeting with Donald Trump at Mar-a-Lago came after the president-elect's threat to impose tariffs on two of America's leading trade partners raised alarms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democratic lawmakers report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: At least five Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut were targeted by bomb threats on Thursday. Police who responded said they found no evidence of explosives on the lawmakers' properties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
